name: Acer Swift Go 14
introText: The Acer Swift Go 14 delivers a 2.8K OLED display, Intel Core Ultra 5, and a 1.25kg chassis for under $900 CAD — the most display quality per dollar in any laptop under $1,000.
whatWorks: 2.8K OLED at under $900 is the main reason to buy this laptop — the display quality would cost $300–400 more in competing ultrabooks, making it a genuine bargain for display-sensitive users.
Light at 1.25kg for a 14-inch laptop with OLED — balances portability and display quality better than heavier alternatives.
worthKnowing: 10-hour battery life is at the shorter end for this category — adequate for a school day or office day but requires the charger for longer stretches.
Build quality uses some plastic elements unlike premium metal-chassis competitors — acceptable at the price but noticeable.

commonComplaints.title: Short battery compared to IPS alternatives
commonComplaints.body: The OLED display draws more power; real-world battery runs 8–10 hours vs. 12–14 for IPS alternatives at the same price.

commonComplaints.title: Webcam quality is average
commonComplaints.body: The 1080p webcam produces acceptable but not impressive image quality for video calls.

commonComplaints.title: Speakers are mediocre
commonComplaints.body: The built-in speakers are thin and lack bass — the beautiful display isn't matched by audio quality.

verdict: The Acer Swift Go 14 is the best laptop under $1,000 if you prioritize display quality — the 2.8K OLED at this price point is extraordinary. Battery life and build quality are the trade-offs. For most users, the display upgrade over IPS alternatives is worth it.

realBuyers.quote: Bought it specifically for the OLED screen. It's stunning. Best laptop display I've ever used under $1,000.
Battery gets me 8-9 hours. Good for my school day but I do need to charge after classes.
Amazing value. An OLED laptop at this price was unthinkable a few years ago.

metaDescription: Acer Swift Go 14 review Canada: Intel Core Ultra 5, 2.8K OLED, 1.25kg. ~$899 CAD. Best OLED laptop under $1,000 for Canadians.
